_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Emergency with unstable cardiopulmonary status with resuscitation
clinical_indications: Unstable Angina (UA): A clinical syndrome subset of Acute coronary syndrome (ACS) defined by ECG ST-segment depression or prominent T wave inversion.

clinical_thresholds.note: no elevation of cardiac biomarkers of necrosis for Unstable Angina (UA)

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Clinical notes and ECG report indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers with vitals and treatment details submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary and treatment details provided including drugs used for thrombolysis (if applicable) submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes not submitted or incomplete, failing to support Unstable Angina/NSTEMI criteria.
ECG report not submitted or does not show ST-segment depression or prominent T wave inversion for Unstable Angina/NSTEMI.
Cardiac biomarkers (Troponins T/I or CPKMB) are documented as elevated, contradicting the diagnosis of Unstable Angina.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers are missing or lack essential vital and treatment details.
Discharge summary is missing or incomplete, or fails to detail drugs used for thrombolysis (if applicable).

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for treatment only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination.
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: Load with clopidogrel or prasugrel or ticagrelor if not already done.
additional_information.post_op_care: Continue Dual anti-platelets in patients undergoing PCI for at least 12 months in DES and for 3 months in BMS.
additional_information.fitness_criteria: Very High risk clinical instability: Immediate invasive (<2 hours); High risk (GRACE >140, TIMI >4): Early invasive (2-24 hours); Intermediate risk (GRACE 109-140, TIMI 2-3): Delayed invasive (25-72 hours); Low risk (GRACE <109, TIMI <=1): Medical/non-invasive strategy or non-invasive ischaemic testing.
additional_information.comorbidity_management: Optimal medical therapy to continue (BB, high dose atorvastatin, ACE-inhibitors, intra-venous nitrates if ongoing pain, severe MR or LVF).
additional_information.referral_criteria: Refer as emergency to nearest Primary PCI/Thrombolysis capable centre for Red Flag Signs (pain >20 mins, recurrent pain, breathlessness, profuse sweating, syncope, hemodynamic instability). Refer High/Intermediate risk to PCI capable centre. Refer to PCI capable centre if Acute LVF, Hypotension, Systolic murmur, Arrhythmia.
contraindications: Angina unlikely if: Variable location or characteristic of pain
Angina unlikely if: Long lasting (hours to days) or short lasting (less than a minute) pain
Angina unlikely if: Pain restricted to areas above jaw or below epigastrium
Angina unlikely if: Localized to a point pain
Angina unlikely if: Pricking or piercing or stabbing type of pain
Angina unlikely if: Pain precipitated by movement of neck or arms or respiration
